Name: spectral_stability_bounds_latency_inference
Title: Spectral Stability Bounds for Training-Free Latency-Aware Inference
Short Hypothesis: Spectral stability bounds derived from activation covariance geometry can serve as zero-overhead proxies for inference difficulty, enabling dynamic compute throttling to enforce strict latency budgets without compromising accuracy.
Related Work: Existing approaches like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) and Geometric Interference Cancellation focus on specific aspects of training-free inference but do not explicitly link spectral stability to latency-aware compute allocation. SQR uses spectral decay for precision allocation, while Geometric Interference Cancellation isolates task subspaces. Our proposal bridges these ideas by introducing spectral stability bounds as a unified framework for latency-aware inference.
Abstract: We propose a training-free framework that leverages spectral stability bounds derived from activation covariance geometry to dynamically allocate computational resources during inference. By computing the condition number and spectral decay rate of hidden states on-the-fly, we establish a direct link between spectral stability and inference difficulty. Tokens residing in well-conditioned subspaces are routed through fewer layers or pruned attention heads, enforcing strict latency budgets without compromising accuracy. We provide theoretical PAC-Bayes bounds linking spectral stability to generalization under perturbation, offering a transparent pathway for resource-efficient inference. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across adversarial benchmarks and natural domain shifts, our method achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic baselines.
Experiments: Dynamic Compute Throttling: Implement a scheduler that routes tokens through fewer layers or prunes attention heads based on spectral stability thresholds. Evaluate latency and accuracy on WILDS benchmarks.
Spectral Stability Metrics: Compute condition number and spectral decay rate of hidden states using randomized SVD. Validate these metrics as proxies for inference difficulty.
PAC-Bayes Generalization Bounds: Derive theoretical bounds linking spectral stability to generalization under perturbation. Validate these bounds empirically across adversarial benchmarks.
Throughput-Accuracy Pareto Frontier: Compare our method to static and heuristic baselines, measuring throughput (tokens/second) and accuracy retention under natural domain shifts.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Overhead of Randomized SVD: The computational cost of randomized SVD may offset latency gains. Mitigate this by using lightweight approximations.
Generalization Across Tasks: Spectral stability metrics may not generalize well across diverse tasks. Validate across multiple benchmarks.
Hardware Constraints: The method assumes single-GPU setups. Extend to multi-GPU environments for broader applicability.
